examples.en: Rising inflation made everyday groceries more expensive this year.
examples.zh: 上升的通膨讓今年的日常食品雜貨變得更貴。

examples.en: The central bank raised rates to fight inflation.
examples.zh: 央行升息以對抗通膨。

img: grocery prices rising on a chart

examples.en: When the supply of coffee drops, prices usually go up.
examples.zh: 當咖啡的供給減少時,價格通常會上漲。

examples.en: Farmers increased the supply of rice after a good harvest.
examples.zh: 豐收後農民增加了稻米的供應量。

img: warehouse full of stacked goods

examples.en: Demand for umbrellas rises during the rainy season.
examples.zh: 雨季期間對雨傘的需求會上升。

examples.en: High demand for tickets sold out the concert in minutes.
examples.zh: 門票的高需求讓演唱會在幾分鐘內售罄。

img: crowd of shoppers reaching for products

examples.en: A chip shortage slowed down car production worldwide.
examples.zh: 晶片短缺拖慢了全球的汽車生產。

examples.en: The drought caused a serious water shortage in the city.
examples.zh: 乾旱造成該城市嚴重的缺水。

img: empty supermarket shelves

examples.en: The factory had a surplus of goods it could not sell.
examples.zh: 工廠有一批賣不掉的過剩商品。

examples.en: A trade surplus means a country exports more than it imports.
examples.zh: 貿易順差意味著一國出口大於進口。

img: overflowing warehouse of extra boxes

examples.en: Many people lost their jobs during the recession.
examples.zh: 許多人在經濟衰退期間失去了工作。

examples.en: The economy slowly recovered after the recession ended.
examples.zh: 經濟在衰退結束後緩慢復甦。

img: downward red arrow over city skyline

examples.en: Economic growth created many new jobs last year.
examples.zh: 經濟成長去年創造了許多新工作。

examples.en: The company reported strong growth in overseas sales.
examples.zh: 該公司報告海外銷售強勁成長。

img: green bar chart climbing upward

examples.en: A strong economy helps families feel more secure.
examples.zh: 強勁的經濟讓家庭感到更有保障。

examples.en: The country's economy depends heavily on tourism.
examples.zh: 這個國家的經濟高度依賴觀光業。

img: busy city with factories and shops

examples.en: Unemployment rose sharply after the factory closed.
examples.zh: 工廠關閉後失業率急遽上升。

examples.en: The government offers training to reduce unemployment.
examples.zh: 政府提供訓練以降低失業。

img: person holding empty job application

examples.en: New factories brought employment to the small town.
examples.zh: 新工廠為這個小鎮帶來就業機會。

examples.en: Full employment means almost everyone has a job.
examples.zh: 充分就業意味著幾乎每個人都有工作。

img: workers entering an office building

examples.en: The workers asked for a higher hourly wage.
examples.zh: 工人要求更高的時薪。

examples.en: Minimum wage laws protect low-income workers.
examples.zh: 最低工資法保護低收入工作者。

img: hand receiving cash from paycheck

examples.en: The factory needs skilled labor to run the machines.
examples.zh: 工廠需要熟練的勞動力來操作機器。

examples.en: A shortage of labor slowed down the harvest.
examples.zh: 勞動力短缺拖慢了收成。

img: line of construction workers with tools

examples.en: She bought some stock in a well-known tech company.
examples.zh: 她買了一家知名科技公司的股票。

examples.en: The price of the stock jumped after the good news.
examples.zh: 利多消息後股票價格大漲。

img: stock ticker board with green numbers

examples.en: He owns a small share of the family business.
examples.zh: 他持有家族企業的一小部分股份。

examples.en: The value of each share rose by ten dollars.
examples.zh: 每股的價值上漲了十美元。

img: pie chart split into ownership slices

examples.en: Every shareholder was invited to the annual meeting.
examples.zh: 每位股東都受邀參加年度會議。

examples.en: Shareholders voted to keep the current president.
examples.zh: 股東們投票留任現任總裁。

img: people raising hands at a company meeting

examples.en: The company pays a dividend to investors every quarter.
examples.zh: 該公司每季發放股息給投資人。

examples.en: A steady dividend attracts long-term shareholders.
examples.zh: 穩定的股息能吸引長期股東。

img: coins dropping into an investor's hand

examples.en: The investor put her savings into the stock market.
examples.zh: 這位投資人把積蓄投入股市。

examples.en: Smart investors spread their money across many stocks.
examples.zh: 聰明的投資人會把資金分散到許多股票上。

img: person studying charts on a laptop

examples.en: Trade between the two countries grew quickly.
examples.zh: 兩國之間的貿易迅速成長。

examples.en: Free trade allows goods to move without heavy taxes.
examples.zh: 自由貿易讓貨物流通不必課重稅。

img: cargo ships at a busy port

examples.en: Rice is one of the country's main exports.
examples.zh: 稻米是這個國家的主要出口品之一。

examples.en: Exports fell when foreign demand dropped.
examples.zh: 當國外需求下降時,出口也減少了。

img: shipping containers loaded onto a ship

examples.en: Oil is a major import for many countries.
examples.zh: 石油是許多國家的主要進口品。

examples.en: The tax on imports made foreign cars more expensive.
examples.zh: 進口稅讓外國車變得更貴。

img: truck unloading foreign goods at customs

examples.en: Consumers spend more when they feel confident.
examples.zh: 消費者有信心時會花更多錢。

examples.en: Higher prices hurt the average consumer.
examples.zh: 更高的物價傷害了一般消費者。

img: shopper pushing a full grocery cart

examples.en: Local producers sell their vegetables at the market.
examples.zh: 本地生產者在市場上販售蔬菜。

examples.en: Oil producers control much of the world's energy.
examples.zh: 石油生產者掌控了世界大部分的能源。

img: farmer harvesting crops in a field

examples.en: The factory doubled its output after adding new machines.
examples.zh: 工廠增添新機器後產量翻倍。

examples.en: Falling output is often a sign of a weak economy.
examples.zh: 產量下滑往往是經濟疲弱的徵兆。

img: assembly line producing many products

examples.en: New tools improved the team's productivity.
examples.zh: 新工具提升了團隊的生產力。

examples.en: Higher productivity lets workers make more in less time.
examples.zh: 更高的生產力讓工人在更短時間內產出更多。

img: efficient factory with fast conveyor belt

examples.en: The housing boom made many builders rich.
examples.zh: 房市榮景讓許多建商致富。

examples.en: A tech boom brought thousands of jobs to the city.
examples.zh: 科技榮景為這座城市帶來數千個工作。

img: fireworks over a rising skyline

examples.en: Car sales fell into a slump during the crisis.
examples.zh: 危機期間汽車銷售陷入低迷。

examples.en: The long slump forced many shops to close.
examples.zh: 長期的低迷迫使許多店家歇業。

img: sharp downward line on a graph

examples.en: The teacher drew a demand curve on the board.
examples.zh: 老師在黑板上畫了一條需求曲線。

examples.en: The demand curve shows how price affects buying.
examples.zh: 需求曲線顯示價格如何影響購買。

img: sloping line graph on a whiteboard

examples.en: The price of gas keeps changing every week.
examples.zh: 汽油的價格每週都在變動。

examples.en: When demand rises, the price often goes up.
examples.zh: 當需求上升時,價格往往會上漲。

img: price tag hanging on a product

examples.en: The cost of raw materials went up this year.
examples.zh: 原物料的成本今年上漲了。

examples.en: Rising costs cut into the company's profit.
examples.zh: 上升的成本侵蝕了公司的利潤。

img: calculator next to stacks of receipts

examples.en: The company hopes to enter the Asian market.
examples.zh: 這家公司希望進入亞洲市場。

examples.en: A free market lets prices adjust on their own.
examples.zh: 自由市場讓價格自行調整。

img: busy trading floor with many people

examples.en: Competition between airlines lowered ticket prices.
examples.zh: 航空公司之間的競爭降低了票價。

examples.en: Strong competition pushes companies to improve.
examples.zh: 激烈的競爭促使公司不斷改進。

img: two runners racing side by side

examples.en: The firm had a monopoly on the local water supply.
examples.zh: 該公司壟斷了當地的供水。

examples.en: A monopoly can charge high prices without competition.
examples.zh: 獨佔企業在沒有競爭下可以索取高價。

img: one giant company controlling a market

examples.en: The dollar is a widely used currency around the world.
examples.zh: 美元是世界上廣泛使用的貨幣。

examples.en: A weak currency makes imports more expensive.
examples.zh: 疲弱的貨幣讓進口商品變得更貴。

img: different banknotes from many countries

examples.en: The value of the house rose over the years.
examples.zh: 這棟房子的價值多年來上升了。

examples.en: Inflation lowers the value of your savings.
examples.zh: 通膨會降低你存款的價值。

img: coin with an upward value arrow

examples.en: The new car was her biggest purchase this year.
examples.zh: 這輛新車是她今年最大的一筆購買。

examples.en: Consumers delayed big purchases during the slump.
examples.zh: 消費者在景氣低迷時延後了大筆消費。

img: customer paying at a checkout counter

examples.en: Consumer spending drives much of the economy.
examples.zh: 消費者支出推動了大部分的經濟。

examples.en: Families cut their spending when prices rose.
examples.zh: 物價上漲時家庭削減了支出。

img: wallet with money flowing out

examples.en: The economic downturn hurt small businesses the most.
examples.zh: 經濟下滑對小型企業的傷害最大。

examples.en: Sales dropped during the downturn.
examples.zh: 在景氣下滑期間銷售下降。

img: declining graph with a red line

examples.en: The economy showed signs of recovery last spring.
examples.zh: 經濟在去年春天顯現復甦的跡象。

examples.en: A slow recovery brought jobs back little by little.
examples.zh: 緩慢的復甦讓工作機會一點一點回來。

img: green sprout growing from coins

examples.en: The stock index rose two percent today.
examples.zh: 股市指數今天上漲了百分之二。

examples.en: A price index tracks how living costs change.
examples.zh: 物價指數追蹤生活成本如何變化。

img: stock index number on a screen

examples.en: The unemployment rate fell to a five-year low.
examples.zh: 失業率降到五年來的低點。

examples.en: A high interest rate makes borrowing costly.
examples.zh: 高利率使借錢的成本變高。

img: percentage sign on an economic dashboard

examples.en: Factories produce goods for people to buy.
examples.zh: 工廠生產供人們購買的商品。

examples.en: The price of everyday goods keeps rising.
examples.zh: 日常商品的價格持續上漲。

img: shelves stocked with various products

examples.en: The government used demand-side policies to boost spending.
examples.zh: 政府運用需求面政策來刺激消費。

examples.en: Demand-side pressure pushed prices higher.
examples.zh: 需求面的壓力推高了物價。

img: arrows pointing toward buyers in a diagram

examples.en: The economic forecast points to slower growth.
examples.zh: 經濟預測指向成長趨緩。

examples.en: Analysts raised their forecast for next year.
examples.zh: 分析師調高了對明年的預測。

img: analyst pointing at a future trend chart

examples.en: The upward trend in prices worried shoppers.
examples.zh: 物價上升的趨勢讓購物者擔憂。

examples.en: Economists study long-term trends in the market.
examples.zh: 經濟學家研究市場的長期趨勢。

img: arrow following a rising line on a chart

examples.en: Years of prosperity raised the whole town's living standard.
examples.zh: 多年的繁榮提升了整個城鎮的生活水準。

examples.en: Trade brought prosperity to the coastal region.
examples.zh: 貿易為沿海地區帶來繁榮。

img: thriving city street full of shoppers

examples.en: The scarcity of clean water raised its price.
examples.zh: 乾淨水源的稀缺推高了它的價格。

examples.en: Scarcity of resources forces people to make choices.
examples.zh: 資源的稀缺迫使人們做出選擇。

img: single water drop above dry cracked ground

examples.en: The country's wealth grew as its exports rose.
examples.zh: 隨著出口上升,這個國家的財富增加了。

examples.en: Education helps people build long-term wealth.
examples.zh: 教育幫助人們建立長期的財富。

img: growing pile of gold coins and property

examples.en: Their monthly income barely covers the rent.
examples.zh: 他們的月收入勉強夠付房租。

examples.en: Higher wages raised the family's income.
examples.zh: 更高的工資提高了這家庭的收入。

img: monthly paycheck deposited into an account

examples.en: Gross domestic product measures a country's total output.
examples.zh: 國內生產毛額衡量一國的總產出。

examples.en: The company's gross income was higher than expected.
examples.zh: 公司的毛收入高於預期。

img: large total sum highlighted on a report

examples.en: Domestic sales grew faster than exports this year.
examples.zh: 今年國內銷售的成長比出口更快。

examples.en: The policy aims to protect domestic industries.
examples.zh: 這項政策旨在保護國內產業。

img: map of one country highlighted in color
